methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Hertha Metals is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $17M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 6 private companies at Series B stage within the same sector, supplemented by reference to 1 public comparable and a broader private market dataset of 8 robotics firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $17M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage progression, yielding $34M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the six same-sector comparables: 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M). The median of this peer set is $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B, reflecting substantial dispersion in the robotics subsector.

Triangulating these methods produces a low estimate of $29M, a median of $554M, and a high of $1.2B. The wide range reflects the dual challenges: stage-progression yields a conservative floor given early-stage comparables, while peer medians capture heterogeneous outcomes across Series B robotics firms. Key uncertainties include Hertha's relative technical differentiation versus peers, capital efficiency trajectory, and customer traction—factors not directly observable in valuation data but material to outcomes within this $29M-$1.2B band.
